llm_operation.reason: PyTorch CTCLoss is not a callable loss function that takes only input logits; it requires mandatory positional arguments: targets, input_lengths, and target_lengths. The original test case incorrectly treats it as a stateless function accepting only input tensor. TensorFlow does not have tf.keras.losses.CTCloss — the correct API is tf.nn.ctc_loss (a function, not a Keras Loss class), which also requires logits, labels, label_length, and logit_length. Both frameworks require full CTC alignment inputs (logits, targets, and length tensors), so the test case must be repaired to provide all required arguments with valid shapes and semantics per documentation.
